mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate reduces the discount rate on future earnings and supports equity valuations, so a 20-day S&P 500 return is positive when policy is easing and the rate level is declining. The signal fails if rate cuts occur during acute growth deterioration and equities sell off instead of repricing higher.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive more often than negative during declining FEDFUNDS periods, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE indicates broadening labor income and household purchasing power, which lifts expected sales and supports S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when job gains are concentrated but unemployment does not improve, signaling weak demand transmission.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 100k in a month and UNRATE falls or holds flat
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not deliver positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if PAYEMS gains without UNRATE improvement show no return premium.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS reflects stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal fails if retail sales improve only because of inflation while real activity and market breadth weaken.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS is followed by negative 60-day returns in a majority of cases, or if rolling 3-year IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and tighter real-economy momentum, which should pressure cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when output softness is already fully priced and equities rally on easing-rate expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal’s hit rate falls below baseline over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains low implies inflation is outpacing policy reaction, raising the probability of future tightening and discount-rate pressure on equities. S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days when inflation surprises are not offset by restrictive policy. The mechanism fails if higher CPI is accompanied by stable or falling inflation expectations and no repricing of rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month over month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation spikes are not followed by negative 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the rolling Rank IC is above zero despite persistent CPI acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST indicates stronger housing demand and a healthier interest-sensitive sector, which transmits into consumer confidence and broader equity performance over the next month. The effect fails if housing strength is driven by supply bottlenecks rather than end-demand, in which case equities do not reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if higher HOUST does not correspond to positive 20-day returns in the majority of observations, or if the rolling 3-year IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS falls, labor-market deterioration signals weaker household income and lower future consumption, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails if unemployment rises for benign reasons such as labor-force reentry while payrolls stay resilient.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor deterioration episodes are followed by positive 5-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS rate with CPIAUCSL trending down supports real-rate relief and reduces pressure for additional tightening, which should lift S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. The signal fails in recessionary deflation scares, where falling inflation coincides with risk-off equity selling.
failure_condition: Reject if low FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 20-day returns versus baseline, or if the return premium disappears during rolling 3-year windows.
